article_title: Novel glutamatergic drugs for the treatment of mood disorders

chunks_extracted.section: Glutamatergic neurotransmission as an emerging therapeutic target in mood disorders

aggregated_relations.subject: Metabotropic glutamate receptors
evidence_sentences: Metabotropic glutamate receptors all initiate signal transduction cascades via G-protein coupling and are divided into three groups: Group I (mGluR1 and mGluR5), Group II (mGluR2 and mGluR3), and Group III (mGluR4, mGluR6, mGluR7, mGluR8).

aggregated_relations.subject: Excessive levels of glutamate
evidence_sentences: Excessive levels of glutamate result in the well described phenomenon for neuronal excitotoxicity.

aggregated_relations.subject: calcium-dependent release of glutamate
aggregated_relations.object: binding to ionotropic and metabotropic receptors
evidence_sentences: Glutamate is packaged into synaptic vesicles for calcium-dependent release, which enables binding to ionotropic and metabotropic receptors (see Figure 1).

aggregated_relations.subject: glutamate
evidence_sentences: Excessive levels of glutamate result in the well described phenomenon for neuronal excitotoxicity.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Ketamine is classified as a dissociative anesthetic and produces an anesthetic state characterized by preserved pharyngeal reflexes and cardiovascular and respiratory stimulation when administered intravenously (IV) at doses of 1–3 mg/kg.

Ketamine is classified as a dissociative anesthetic and produces an anesthetic state characterized by preserved pharyngeal reflexes and cardiovascular and respiratory stimulation when administered intravenously (IV) at doses of 1–3 mg/kg.20

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: These findings were also extended to BPD; ketamine added to a mood stabilizer exerted a rapid antidepressant effect in BPD patients who were in a refractory depressive episode at the time of randomization.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Recently, ketamine was found to rapidly increase synaptic density and signaling in cortical neurons in conjunction with rapid antidepressant effects in animal models and to reverse stress-induced trophic deficits following chronic mild stress.

aggregated_relations.subject: Riluzole
evidence_sentences: Riluzole reduces extra-synaptic glutamate by (1) inhibiting glutamate release via presynaptic inhibition of voltage-gated sodium channels, (2) enhancing astroglial uptake of glutamate, and (3) increasing AMPA receptor trafficking.

aggregated_relations.subject: Lamotrigine
evidence_sentences: Lamotrigine inhibits voltage-dependent sodium channels, calcium channels, and potassium channels;44 this is thought to decrease glutamate release and increase the AMPA receptor expression.

aggregated_relations.subject: Lamotrigine

aggregated_relations.subject: Lamotrigine
evidence_sentences: Lamotrigine also weakly inhibits serotonin reuptake45 and may exert neuroprotective effects.
Lamotrigine also weakly inhibits serotonin reuptake45 and may exert neuroprotective effects.46,47

aggregated_relations.subject: Lamotrigine
evidence_sentences: In rats subjected to chronic unpredictable stress, chronic administration of lamotrigine ameliorated behavioral deficits in both the sucrose preference test (SPT) and novelty-suppressed feeding test (NSFT).

aggregated_relations.subject: lamotrigine
evidence_sentences: In TRD patients (≥2 antidepressant failures), open-label treatment with lamotrigine resulted in significant reductions in symptoms of depression by 8 weeks and a significant improvement in social and occupational functioning at 6 months.

aggregated_relations.subject: lamotrigine

chunks_extracted.section: NMDA receptor modulators other than ketamine

aggregated_relations.subject: Quinidine
evidence_sentences: Quinidine is a potent inhibitor of the CYP2D6 enzymatic pathway and thereby functions to raise the blood levels of dextromethorphan.

aggregated_relations.subject: GLYX-13
evidence_sentences: GLYX-13 showed potential antidepressant efficacy in rats by reducing immobility on FST and ameliorated symptoms of learned helplessness.

aggregated_relations.subject: GLYX-13
evidence_sentences: GLYX-13 showed potential antidepressant efficacy in rats by reducing immobility on FST and ameliorated symptoms of learned helplessness.

aggregated_relations.subject: Ro25-6981
evidence_sentences: In several preclinical studies, the GluN2B antagonist Ro25-6981 demonstrated antidepressant efficacy by reducing immobility on the FST and increasing sucrose preference following chronic unpredictable stress (CUS).

aggregated_relations.subject: Ro25-6981
evidence_sentences: In several preclinical studies, the GluN2B antagonist Ro25-6981 demonstrated antidepressant efficacy by reducing immobility on the FST and increasing sucrose preference following chronic unpredictable stress (CUS).

aggregated_relations.subject: dextromethorphan
evidence_sentences: In addition to NMDAR antagonism, dextromethorphan is an agonist at mu (μ) and sigma-1 opioid receptors and blocks calcium channels, serotonin transporters, and muscarinic receptors (reviewed by Lauterbach68).

aggregated_relations.subject: dextromethorphan
evidence_sentences: In addition to NMDAR antagonism, dextromethorphan is an agonist at mu (μ) and sigma-1 opioid receptors and blocks calcium channels, serotonin transporters, and muscarinic receptors (reviewed by Lauterbach68).

aggregated_relations.subject: AZD6765
relation_raw_variants: is a low-to-moderate affinity open-channel NMDA antagonist
evidence_sentences: AZD6765 (Lanicemine, AstraZeneca, London, UK) is a low-to-moderate affinity open-channel NMDA antagonist, that has been reported in a RCT to rapidly provide short-lived relief of depressive symptoms without eliciting dissociative or psychotomimetic symptoms in a single-site clinical trial.

causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: In several preclinical studies, the GluN2B antagonist Ro25-6981 demonstrated antidepressant efficacy by reducing immobility on the FST and increasing sucrose preference following chronic unpredictable stress (CUS).

causal_tuples.subject: dextromethorphan
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: In addition to NMDAR antagonism, dextromethorphan is an agonist at mu (μ) and sigma-1 opioid receptors and blocks calcium channels, serotonin transporters, and muscarinic receptors (reviewed by Lauterbach68).

causal_tuples.subject: Quinidine
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Quinidine is a potent inhibitor of the CYP2D6 enzymatic pathway and thereby functions to raise the blood levels of dextromethorphan.

causal_tuples.subject: AZD6765
causal_tuples.relation_raw: is a low-to-moderate affinity open-channel NMDA antagonist
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: AZD6765 (Lanicemine, AstraZeneca, London, UK) is a low-to-moderate affinity open-channel NMDA antagonist, that has been reported in a RCT to rapidly provide short-lived relief of depressive symptoms without eliciting dissociative or psychotomimetic symptoms in a single-site clinical trial.

aggregated_relations.subject: AMPA-PAMs
evidence_sentences: AMPA receptor (AMPAR) positive allosteric modulators (AMPA-PAMs; also known as AMPA potentiators or ‘AMPAkines’) slow the rate of AMPAR deactivation or desensitization.

aggregated_relations.subject: AMPAR signaling
evidence_sentences: Interest in these agents as potential antidepressants stems in part from the observation that traditional antidepressants regulate AMPARs83,84 and the ability of signaling through AMPARs to increase BDNF,85,86 in addition to glutamatergic effects.

aggregated_relations.subject: LY392098
evidence_sentences: LY392098 and LY451646 – biarylsulphonamide AMPAR potentiators – reduced immobility in the forced swim and tail suspension tests (FST, TST), and LY392098’s effects were blocked by an AMPAR antagonist.

aggregated_relations.subject: aniracetam
evidence_sentences: In a less common model of depression, two benzoylpyrrolidinones (aniracetam and piracetam) and three benzoylpyrrolidines (Ampakines; CX516, CX691, and CX731) all reduced submissive behavior regarding a rewarding food source.

aggregated_relations.subject: EAATs
evidence_sentences: Glutamate uptake by EAATs is the primary means by which glutamate is removed from the extracellular space, thereby limiting the potential for excitotoxicity caused by excessive glutamate signaling.

aggregated_relations.subject: acamprosate
evidence_sentences: For example, acamprosate, used for the treatment of alcohol abuse, which also has antagonist activity at both NMDAR and mGluR5 receptors, has shown antidepressant-like activity on the TST116 along with trends but not statistically significant improvements in underpowered studies of depressive symptoms in patients with comorbid depression and alcohol dependence.117,118

aggregated_relations.subject: acamprosate
evidence_sentences: For example, acamprosate, used for the treatment of alcohol abuse, which also has antagonist activity at both NMDAR and mGluR5 receptors, has shown antidepressant-like activity on the TST116 along with trends but not statistically significant improvements in underpowered studies of depressive symptoms in patients with comorbid depression and alcohol dependence.117,118

aggregated_relations.subject: memantine
evidence_sentences: Memantine (Namenda) is an amantadine derivative and voltage-dependent non-competitive low-affinity NMDAR antagonist, and is approved for the treatment of moderate-to-severe Alzheimer’s disease.119
